title: Windows: ADD https://<giturl> fails due to missing `git` in `podman-machine-default`
body:
### Issue Description

The `podman-machine-default` misses `git` for Windows (probably MacOS as well). Thus you cannot use the 
```
ADD https://<domain>/<name>/repo.git
```
syntax in a `Containerfile`/`Dockerfile`. Seen with Podman version 5.7.1 .

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install Podman 5.7.1 on windows
2. `podman machine init`
3. `podman machine start`
4. create a `Containerfile`/`Dockerfile`
```Dockerfile
FROM fedora:42
ADD https://github.com/containers/podman.git /checkout
```
5. `podman build --tag testme .`


### Describe the results you received

```
STEP 1/2: FROM fedora:42
STEP 2/2: ADD https://github.com/containers/podman.git /checkout
Error: building at STEP "ADD https://github.com/containers/podman.git /checkout": reading "https://github.com/containers/podman.git": cloning "https://github.com/containers/podman.git" to "/var/tmp/1017777799":
: failed while performing `git init`: exec: "git": executable file not found in $PATH
```

### Describe the results you expected

The repository should be checked out.

### podman info output

```yaml
podman version 5.7.1 on Windows 11
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

The problem is similar to https://github.com/containers/image_build/issues/49

#### Workaround - Variant 1
```
podman machine ssh
su -
dnf install git-core
```
#### Workaround - Variant 2
use a ansible playbook in conjunction with `podman machine init`
```bash
cat << EOF > playbook.yml
---
- name: Provision podman-machine-default
  hosts: localhost
  become: yes
  tasks:
    - name: Install git
      ansible.builtin.package:
        name:
          - git-core
          - git-lfs
      changed_when: false
EOF
podman machine init --playbook playbook.yml
```

comments.body:
Thanks @userid0x0 ,

I have opened https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/pull/217 to try and resolve this!

Apologize my question.
When is this fix active?

I did
* a `podman machine reset` 
* double checked that the `.local/share/containers` folder is empty on my windows machine.

A new `podman machine init` seems to download the `podman-machine-os` - but this seems to be the _old_ image without `git-core`.
```
Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.7 to create VM
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a88eaedde7e2 done   |
Copying config 44136fa355 done   |

a88eaedde7e2006df5c34894695ebd1d6beda57d92a4492a5a670ed20609cdf1

The operation completed successfully.
```

But quay.io has the new image online https://quay.io/repository/podman/machine-os/manifest/sha256:dc1add439a53bdd6e2bcd4fa164f57bd63e0c3f9c6fd49c4d357cd50d4af830d

There seems to be an inconsistency

```
Writing manifest to image destination
git version 2.51.1
```
but
```
podman machine ssh git --version
bash: line 1: git: command not found
```

The podman machine run downloads a image with multiple layers - afaik the `podman machine init` seems to use a single layer image (single tar) for the wsl import. Are these not in sync?

The machine image is tied to the podman CLI version be used it's like an appliance and the versions should match. 

So when the next release of Podman is cut, a new image will be officially tagged and your steps would pull it in. 

In your example your consuming the 5.7 image, this won't contain the fix. 

As you saw, the image is on quay so you can try the :next tag or consume the image from my PR: https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/pull/217#issuecomment-3847654826

Makes sense - just wanted to check back.
Thanks for the great support.
